Items.Title: 介绍 Lightship 0.1.0 | Introducting Lightship 0.1.0
Items.Description:
您好，欢迎阅读我的新文章，我想在这里展示我最新的开源项目：Lightship PHP。

  • 它是如何开始的
  • 怀疑
  • 使用示例
  • 下一步是什么？

它是如何开始的

这一切都始于一条推文，我在推文中询问 Laravel 社区，人们是否会对命令行工具感兴趣，以便为他们的路线运行 Google Lighthouse 报告。

我为自己的应用程序制作了这个，它缺乏细节，沉重的 WIP 🚧

这条推文刺激了评论部分，它让我有意愿去开始为社区创建一个包。

怀疑

我喜欢挑战自己，就好像我是这个包的用户一样。 所以我问自己这个包是否易于使用，结果我花了几个小时努力安装无头谷歌浏览器，并设置它以便我可以从我的包中调用它（我使用 Docker）。

这是让我重新思考这个项目的成功的第一件事。

和我的兄弟阿明聊了一会儿，因为我们有很好的讨论，这些聊天总是给我很好的反馈，让我不断挑战自己，我们开始想象：

如果我们不必使用 Web 浏览器来进行网页审核会怎样？

所以我开始摆弄一些使用纯 HTTP 调用的原型，并开始获得一些不错的结果。 我正要完成第一个工作版本，当我在 Discord 上将它呈现给我的兄弟时，这就是启示。

审核非常快，我们开始创建一个组织（链接在文章末尾），以及一个 RFC 和一些 Node 和 PHP 的实现。

使用示例

我坚持使用 PHP，因为我的最终目标是拥有一个工具来快速审核我的 Laravel 公共路由，以确保我有尽可能高的分数来优化我的排名（关于我正在烘焙的项目的另一篇 Dev.to 文章即将发布）。

安装和使用非常简单。

安装包

首先，首先要求将包放入您的项目中：

 composer require lightship-core/lightship-php

创建脚本

创建一个 PHP 文件，并插入以下代码：

 use Lightship\Lightship ; require __DIR__ . "/vendor/autoload.php" ; $lightship = new Lightship (); $lightship -> route ( "https://example.com" ) -> route ( "https://google.com" ) -> route ( "https://news.google.com" ) -> analyse (); file_put_contents ( "report.json" , $lightship -> toPrettyJson ());

执行脚本

现在，只需在您的终端上运行此命令，几秒钟后，将出现一个新文件“report.json”，其中包含每个 URL 的分数和结果。

The "report.json" looks like this: “report.json”看起来像这样：

 [ { "url" : "https: \/\/ example.com" , "durationInSeconds" : 0.32 , "scores" : { "seo" : 50 , "security" : 25 , "performance" : 100 , "accessibility" : 50 }, "seo" : [ { "name" : "titlePresent" , "passes" : true }, { "name" : "langPresent" , "passes" : false }, ... ], "security" : [ { "name" : "xFrameOptionsPresent" , "passes" : false }, { "name" : "strictTransportSecurityHeaderPresent" , "passes" : false }, ... ], "performance" : [ { "name" : "textCompressionEnabled" , "passes" : true }, { "name" : "noRedirects" , "passes" : true }, ... ], "accessibility" : [ { "name" : "metaViewportPresent" , "passes" : true }, { "name" : "useLandmarkTags" , "passes" : false }, ... ] }, ...
]

下一步是什么？

我想继续前进，为你们付出更多！

在接下来的几天里，我将创建一个 Laravel 包，以便能够为您自动执行此操作，并提供一个不错的控制台报告。

您还应该查看 Lightship PHP 的 PHP 存储库，您将了解到：

  • 如何自己运行脚本并显示控制台报告
  • 如何使用具有更多功能（如查询字符串和基本域支持）的 lightship.json 配置文件

您还将发挥重要作用，因为如果没有您的支持和反馈，这个软件包就无法生存。 请在评论中让我知道您的想法，我可以改进什么，或者您的所有建议以使其对 v1 更可行！

如果您想使用它并创建自己的实现，请继续，我将在 RFC README 中引用它。 我认为这是训练你的编程技能的一个很好的方法，我在做 PHP 实现时玩得很开心！

快乐的网络审核🤓



原文:

Hello and welcome to my new article where I would like to showcase my newest open-source project: Lightship PHP.

  • How it started
  • Doubts
  • Usage example
  • What's next?

How it started

It all started from a Tweet, where I was asking the Laravel community if folks would be interesting in a command line tool to facilitate running Google Lighthouse reports for their routes.

#Laravel community, would you be interested? 👀

I made this for my own app, it lacks details, heavy WIP 🚧

This tweet stimulated the comments section and it gave me the will to go and start creating a package for the community.

Doubts

I like to challenge myself, as if I was a user of the package. So I asked myself if the package was easy to use, and turns out I had spent a few hours struggling to install Google Chrome headless, and setting it up so that I could call it from my package (I use Docker).

This was the first thing that made me re-think the success of this project.

After a little chat with my brother Amin, because we have great discusses and these chats always gives me good feedbacks to keep challeging myself, we started to imagine:

What if we did not had to use a web browser to get a web page audit?

So I started fiddling with some prototypes using pure HTTP calls, and it started to get some good results. I was about to wrap up a first working version and when I presented it to my bro on Discord, this was the revelation.

The audits were pretty fast, and we started to create an organization (links at the end of the article), as well as an RFC and some implementations for Node and PHP.

Usage example

I stick to PHP since my end goal is to have a tool to quickly audit my Laravel public routes to be sure I have the best score possible to optimize my ranking (another Dev.to article on the project I am baking is coming).

Lightship PHP is supporting PHP 8.1 and greater. The installation and usage is dead simple.

Install the package

First, start by requiring the package into your project:

composer require lightship-core/lightship-php

Create the script

Create a PHP file, and insert this code:

use Lightship\Lightship; require __DIR__ . "/vendor/autoload.php"; $lightship = new Lightship(); $lightship->route("https://example.com") ->route("https://google.com") ->route("https://news.google.com") ->analyse(); file_put_contents("report.json", $lightship->toPrettyJson());

Execute the script

Now, just run this command on your terminal, and after a few seconds a new file "report.json" will appear with the scores and results for each of your URLs.

The "report.json" looks like this:

[ { "url": "https:\/\/example.com", "durationInSeconds": 0.32, "scores": { "seo": 50, "security": 25, "performance": 100, "accessibility": 50 }, "seo": [ { "name": "titlePresent", "passes": true }, { "name": "langPresent", "passes": false }, ... ], "security": [ { "name": "xFrameOptionsPresent", "passes": false }, { "name": "strictTransportSecurityHeaderPresent", "passes": false }, ... ], "performance": [ { "name": "textCompressionEnabled", "passes": true }, { "name": "noRedirects", "passes": true }, ... ], "accessibility": [ { "name": "metaViewportPresent", "passes": true }, { "name": "useLandmarkTags", "passes": false }, ... ] }, ...
]

What's next?

I would like to keep going and give more for you guys!

I will create in the next days a Laravel package to be able to automatize this for you, with a nice console report.

You should also take a look at the PHP repository for Lightship PHP, you will learn:

  • How to run a script and display a console report yourself
  • How to use a lightship.json configuration file, with many more features (like query string and base domain support)

You also will play an essential role, because this package can't live without your support and feedbacks. Please let me know here in the comments what do you think, what can I improve, or all your suggestions to make it more viable for a v1!

If you want to play with it and create your own implementation, please go ahead and I will reference it on the RFC README. I think this can be a very good way to train your programming skills, I had so much fun doing the PHP implementation!

PHP repository: https://github.com/lightship-core/lightship-php
RFC repository: https://github.com/lightship-core/lightship-rfc

Happy web audit 🤓

Items.Description:
萨拉科·奥卢瓦森

发表于为了让您的移动应用程序成功，您需要确保它能够覆盖尽可能广泛的受众。当更多人可以访问您的应用程序时，它将提高您的下载率并最终增加您的总利润。但是为了你的应用程序要覆盖所有人，它必须是可访问的。 那么究竟什么是移动可访问性？​

根据 Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI)，这意味着您正在使您的移动应用程序更容易被残障用户访问。

世界上大约 15% 的人口患有某种形式的残疾。

谁需要可访问的移动应用程序？
正如我之前所说，可访问的移动应用程序适用于残疾人。

残疾形式

  • 想象
  • 听力
  • 流动性
  • 识字

视力障碍
应对某种视觉障碍的用户在使用不适合他们的移动应用程序时会遇到最大的困难。 色盲、视力丧失，甚至完全失明是三大障碍。

估计有 2.53 亿人患有视力障碍：这里有 3600 万人失明，2.17 亿人患有中度至重度视力障碍……

色盲

挑战

  • 无法区分颜色。
  • 影响 8% 的男性，0.5% 的女性。

如何帮助

  • 使用颜色和图标来传达信息。
  • 更改颜色的选项。
  • 大字体

视力下降

什么都看不见。

如何帮助
屏幕阅读器可以在应用程序TalkBAck（Android）中使用，VoiceOver（iOS）是Android和iOS移动平台提供的屏幕阅读器。

其他平台的屏幕阅读器。

移动浏览器：

  • 桌面浏览器

    • 但是，如果您的应用需要不同的东西，可以使用Semantics 小部件对其进行自定义。

      听力障碍

      用户是否耳聋、有轻微听力障碍或在一定范围内难以听到声音。

      如何帮助
      视频和声音剪辑可以通过可选的转录或字幕来增强。

      行动不便

      身体运动受限的人在浏览移动应用程序时可能会遇到问题。 敲击或其他形式的手势需要可配置，以便用户不受特定手势的约束。

      如何帮助

      • 敲击或其他形式的手势需要可配置，以便用户不受特定手势的约束。
      • 此外，手势永远不应成为强制性事件，因为物理运动限制不应限制应用程序的导航行为。

      按钮（或应用程序中的其他可触摸触发器）的大小也是可配置的。

      识字障碍

      可访问性的一个经常被忽视的方面是学习劣势的人的可访问性，例如（功能性）文盲、（功能性）数字文盲和数字文盲。

      如何帮助
      应用程序可以通过使用简单、简短的句子，并辅以清晰的、文化上可识别的图标来更好地为这些用户服务。 语音反馈也是一种选择。

      结论

      使用您的应用程序吸引更多的受众不仅仅是针对尽可能多的平台（移动、Web、桌面），还包括让所有人都可以访问它们，包括那些有暂时或永久性残疾的人。 原生平台已经提供了可访问性机制，幸运的是， Flutter 也提供了！

      了解有关辅助功能的更多信息

      • 颤振.dev
      • 要了解更多关于颤振的信息，请访问 flutter.dev

原文:

Salako Oluwaseun

Posted on Apr 24

For your mobile app to be successful, you need to make sure that it can reach the widest possible audience.​ When more people have access to your app, it will boost your download rates and ultimately increase your total profits.​ But in order for your app to reach everyone, it has to be accessible. So what exactly is mobile accessibility?​

According to the Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI), it means that you’re making your mobile app more accessible to users with disabilities. ​

Approximately 15% of the world's population lives with some forms of disability.

Who needs an accessible Mobile app?
As I previously stated, accessible mobile applications are for people with disabilities.

Forms of Disability

  • Vision
  • Hearing
  • Mobility
  • Literacy

Vision Disability
Users coping with some kind of visual impairment experience the most difficulty using a mobile application that is not adapted to them. Color blindness, vision loss, or even complete blindness are three major impairments.

An estimated 253 million people live with vision impairment: 36 million are blind and 217 million have moderate to severe vision impairment here...

Color Blindness

Challenges

  • Not being able to differentiate between colors.
  • Affects 8% of male population, 0.5% women.

How to help

  • Use Color and Icons to convey messages.
  • Options to change color.
  • Large fonts

Vision loss

Not being able to see anything.

How to help
Screen readers can be made available in the app TalkBAck(Android), and VoiceOver(iOS) are screen readers provided by Android and iOS mobile platforms.

Screen Readers for other platforms.

Mobile Browsers:

  • iOS - VoiceOver
  • Android - TalkBack

Desktop Browsers

  • MacOS - VoiceOver
  • Windows - JAWs & NVDA

Flutter’s standard widgets generate an accessibility tree automatically. However, if your app needs something different, it can be customized using the Semantics widget.

Hearing Disability

Whether a user is deaf, has minor hearing impairment or has difficulties hearing sounds within a certain range.

How to help
Videos and sound clips can be enhanced with optional transcriptions or captions.

Mobility Disability

People that have physical motor limitations might encounter problems while navigating a mobile application. Tapping or other forms of gestures need to be configurable so that a user is not bound to a specific gesture.

How to help

  • Tapping or other forms of gestures need to be configurable so that a user is not bound to a specific gesture.
  • Also, a gesture should never be a mandatory event since physical motor limitations should not restrict the navigational behavior of an app.

Both Android and iOS offer users the ability to configure shortcuts for frequently used phrases or words, as well as timing between presses on the screen. The size of buttons (or other touchable triggers in apps) are also configurable.

Literacy Disability

An often overlooked aspect of accessibility is accessibility for people with a learning disadvantage, like (functional) illiteracy, (functional) innumeracy, and digital illiteracy.

How to help
Apps can better serve these users by using simple, short sentences, supported by clear, culturally recognizable iconography. Voice feedback is also an option.

Conclusion

Reaching a larger audience with your apps is not just about targeting as many platforms as possible (mobile, web, desktop) but also about making them accessible for everyone, including those with temporary or permanent disabilities. Native platforms already provide accessibility mechanisms, and thankfully, Flutter does too!

Learn more about Accessibility

  • flutter.dev
  • Google Developers
  • Accessibility on iOS

To learn more about flutter head to flutter.dev

Array.shift()

when the end parameter is not defined, then the slice extends to the end of the array

let slicedArr2 = arr.slice(1)
// [2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9]

spliced: slice is used to REMOVE elements from an array.

Reordering:

reverse: used to reverse the array's elements.

sort:

By default, the sort() function sorts values as strings. This works well for strings ("Apple" comes before "Banana"). However, if numbers are sorted as strings, "25" is bigger than "100", because "2" is bigger than "1". Because of this, the sort() method will produce incorrect result when sorting numbers.

You can fix this by providing a compare function.

so without compare function, sort() is not that useful. more about sort:

The sort() method sorts the elements of an array in place and returns the sorted array. The default sort order is ascending, built upon converting the elements into strings, then comparing their sequences of UTF-16 code units values.

at: simple, you pass an index and you get the value in that index.

indexOf: indexOf() is the opposite of at(), you pass a value and get the value's index in the array.

Adding and combining arrays :

concat: used to combine (add together) two or more arrays, non of the combined arrays will be modified, concat() will return a new array instead.

used to represent an array in string format.

there is also toLocalString(), search about it as an exercise and learn the difference between toString() and toLocalString(), if you know, please share the answer in a comment.

flat: when you have a nested arrays and want to pull out all these nested arrays to a new array, flat() is used. flat() doesn't modify the array, it returns a new array instead.

Este post esta orientado para usuarios nuevos de MacOs, quienes vienen de usar windows 10 o distros de Linux. Tal vez has trabajando con windows 10 o linux y por alguna razón terminaste trabajando en MacOS, bueno pues esa es mi historia.

Antes de comenzar, tengo usando MacOS, antes ya sabia las cosas básicas como las de que para copiar debes usar la tecla comando + C, pero tengo mas de medio año usando MacOS monterrey y he notado que faltan muchas funciones básicas, afortunadamente he encontrado varias apps que solucionan esto, veamos.

Go to the English version

Alt + TAB

La función que falta: Usar Comando + TAB funciona horrible y solo trabaja con las apps abiertas, no las ventanas, por suerte tenemos Alt + TAB un programa que emula el mismo funcionamiento que tienes en windows 10 usando la tecla Opción (Alt) + TAB y funciona genial.

funciones Cool :

No gastes dinero en Witch, créeme no lo vale.

Enlace:
Obten Alt + TAB aqui

Rectangle


La función que falta: MacOS no tiene una manera genial de dividir la pantalla con una o más apps y no puedes mover esas ventanas con las flechas del teclado, rectangle soluciona eso.

funciones Cool :

  • Puedes minimizar una ventana al dock usando comando + flecha de abajo
  • Puedes agregar mucha personalización para dividir la pantalla.

Nota: Tiene una versión pro, pero la versión gratis funciona genial.

No gastes dinero en Magnet.

Desventajas:

copyless

Items.Title: 重置 CSS 和 Normalize.css | RESET CSS & Normalize.css
Items.Description:
如果您像我一样，将 CSS（级联样式表）添加到您的项目中是开发网页最有趣的部分之一。 这是工作的一部分，它真正让你为你的项目增添一些色彩，并将其变为现实。 然而，有很多关于网页样式的事情你一开始并没有真正注意到。

例如，以我创建的这个非常基本的 html 文档为例：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <head> <title>Vincent's Webpage</title> </head> <body> <main> <header> <nav> <ul> <li><a href="#">Home</a></li> <li><a href="#">Gallery</a></li> <li><a href="#">Contact</a></li> </ul> </nav> <h1>H1 Heading</h1> <h2>H2 Heading</h2> </header> <section class="info"> <p class="description"> paragraph element <section class="examples"> <ul>Example List <li>listItem1</li> <li>listItem2</li> <li>listItem3</li> </ul> <button>THIS IS A BUTTON</button> <input type="checkbox"> THIS IS A CHECKBOX <select> <option>Select Option 1</option> <option>Select Option 2</option> </select> </main> </body> </html>

快速浏览一下，您可以看到合并了几个不同的 html 元素，例如：标题、超链接、段落、列表项、按钮等。

正如您所期望的，这就是这个非常基本的网页的样子：

但是问问自己这些问题：

  • 为什么标题是粗体的？
  • 为什么 H1 标题的大小与 H2 不同？
  • 当您单击超链接时，为什么它会变成紫色？

我们从来没有在这些元素中添加任何我们自己的 CSS 样式，然而它们在这里，都是自己装扮的，没有我们的帮助。 他们成长地好快。

不要让这几个没有答案的问题在晚上困扰着你。 简短的回答是：浏览器默认值。

你知道不同的浏览器都有自己的默认样式设置吗？ 一开始可能很难说，但看看今天使用的一些流行浏览器之间的并排比较。

某些方面可能更难以在浏览器之间进行区分，例如超链接和标题。 但是，当我们查看某些元素（例如复选框和下拉菜单项）时，这一点会变得更加清晰。 如您所见，这些样式特定于每个单独的浏览器。

可以在此处找到特定浏览器样式的详细列表：

  • 火狐
  • 谷歌浏览器

这些浏览器默认值的目的是，如果我们的开发人员自己不指定它们，则页面呈现更加统一和一致。 但是，如果我们不希望我们的页面包含任何已预设的样式怎么办？ 我们是否必须进入并更改/撤消浏览器为它们设置的每个预设样式更改？ 这似乎有点过分了。 幸运的是，我们可以使用一些方法来提高该过程的效率。 这些方法称为CSS 重置。

有几种方法可以在您的页面上实现 CSS 重置：

第一个是通用选择器：

通用选择器 - 如何使用

在一个新的 css 文件中，我们会以星号 (*) 开头，后跟花括号 {}，如下所示：

这样做是针对页面上的每个元素。 通常使用这种方法，我们会将边距和内边距设置为 0，以删除与内边距和边距相关的任何默认用户代理样式。

 *{ margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }

现在我们添加了这些样式更改，我们需要将其链接到我们的初始 html 文档中，如下所示：

 <head> <title>Vincent's Webpage</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="/styles.css"> </head>

现在它已经被链接了，我们可以看看它是如何影响我们的网页的（我将使用 chrome 来展示任何前进的例子）。

如您所见，与边距和填充相关的所有样式都已设置为 0。这是一次重置许多样式更改的非常简单的方法。

这种方法的主要好处是它是一种删除所有格式的非常快速和简单的方法。 不利的一面是，如果您的网页内容很多，则此重置将适用于页面上的许多元素，这反过来会影响网站的速度。

重置文件的更好方法是单独重置每个元素的样式。 这样，您只在需要时应用重置样式，而不是可能存在的每个元素，这将导致您网站的速度更快。

埃里克迈耶的网站

您可以将其复制到 css 文件中并将其链接到您的 html 文档。

 <head> <title>Vincent's Webpage</title> <link rel="stylesheet" href="/reset.css"> <link rel="stylesheet" href="/styles.css"> </head>

注意：将链接放在您自己的 style.css 链接之前非常重要。 这是因为页面是自上而下呈现的，这意味着您想在添加自己的样式之前重置您的 css。

出于本示例的目的，我们之前的 styles.css 已被注释掉，以显示 reset.css 将对我们的网页执行什么操作。

从那里开始，您可以按照自己的方式设计和设置页面样式。

总结 CSS 重置，它是将 HTML 元素的样式重置为一致基线的有效方法。 这将使开发人员高枕无忧，因为他们知道任何用户通过不同的浏览器查看他们的页面都不会受到影响。 执行 css 重置绝不是必需的，但是当您将来继续开发网页时，这是一种很好的技术，可以为您节省大量时间和头痛。

虽然 CSS 重置是一件值得学习的好东西，但它并非没有自己的缺陷，例如难以/几乎无法调试。 它也是非模块化的，这意味着样式中没有分节符。 这就是Normalize.css发挥作用的地方。

归一化.CSS

尼古拉斯·加拉格尔

乔纳森尼尔

它不会重置所有预设样式，而是保留有用的默认值。

人们希望通过 CSS 重置来使用此工具的原因有很多：

  • 保留默认值
    为了详细说明保留的默认值需要什么， normalize.css 使您不必重新设置您已经使用 css 重置重置的内容。

  • 减少杂乱
    当涉及到你的调试工具时，reset css 通常有一个很长的继承链，这简直是一个绝对的噩梦。

  • 模块化与 css 重置不同，Normalize.css 是模块化的，这意味着您的项目被分成单独的部分，使您更容易选择应用到每个部分的样式。
  • 有据可查这个项目的创建是为了帮助教用户默认情况下不同的浏览器如何呈现他们的元素。 他们的代码的每个部分都有注释来描述其特定于每个浏览器的用途。


这只是一个小片段，向您展示我的意思。 该存储库非常直观，有助于让用户深入了解其工作原理。

支持的浏览器

目前，normalize.css 支持以下浏览器：

  • 铬合金
  • 边缘
  • 火狐 ESR+
  • 互联网浏览器 10+
  • 野生动物园 8+
  • 歌剧

推特
  • 声云
  • 监护人
  • 中等的
  • 助推器

    • 分叉后，您可以将文件位置的链接添加到 index.html，就像我们之前对 reset.css 和 styles.css 文件所做的那样。

    从那里，您有两个选择：

    1. 您可以根据自己的喜好修改 normalize.css 文件中的预设设置。
    2. 保持 normalize.css 不变，并在您自己的 .css 文件中覆盖这些更改。 只需确保将 .css 文件添加到 html 文档中的 normalize.css 文件下即可。

    总结

    重置 CSS重置浏览器用户代理附带的所有样式。 但是，它可能会过度，因为它可能会在样式中进行许多不必要的覆盖。 识别错误可能很困难，而且它是非模块化的。 您可以使用通用选择器重置来创建自己的重置，也可以使用 Eric Meyer 的模板。

    相反，它将保留有用的。 它更正了错误和常见的浏览器不一致，并且有据可查且使用直观。

    综上所述

    使用其中一种不一定比另一种更好，但必须了解它们之间的差异，以便在设计网页时确定您希望采用的路线。 重置 CSS 本质上是一个干净的石板，允许您从头开始自由设置页面样式，而 Normalize.css 有助于将其分解为多个部分以实现更多模块化。

    两者都有助于实现相同的目标 - 让默认样式不碍事，让您可以自由地按照您希望的方式设置页面样式。



    原文:

    If you're like me, adding CSS (cascading style sheets) to your projects is one of the most fun parts of developing a webpage. It's the part of the job that really lets you give your project some color, and bring it to life. However, there are many things about webpage styling that you don't really notice in the beginning.

    For example, take this very basic html document I've created:

    <!DOCTYPE html>
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <title>Vincent's Webpage</title>
    </head>

    <body>
      <main>
        <header>
          <nav>
            <ul>
              <li><a href="#">Home</a></li>
              <li><a href="#">Gallery</a></li>
              <li><a href="#">Contact</a></li>
            </ul>
          </nav>
          <h1>H1 Heading</h1>
          <h2>H2 Heading</h2>
        </header>

        <section class="info">
          <p class="description"> paragraph element
        <section class="examples">
          <ul>Example List
            <li>listItem1</li>
            <li>listItem2</li>
            <li>listItem3</li>
          </ul>
          <button>THIS IS A BUTTON</button>
          <input type="checkbox"> THIS IS A CHECKBOX
          <select>
            <option>Select Option 1</option>
            <option>Select Option 2</option>
          </select>
      </main>

    </body>
  </html>

    From a quick glance you can see that there are few different html elements incorporated, such has: headers, hyperlinks, paragraphs, list items, buttons, etc.

    As you'd expect this is what this very basic webpage would look like:

    But ask yourself these questions:

    • Why are the headers bold?
    • Why is an H1 heading a different size than an H2?
    • When you click a hyperlink, why did it turn purple?

    We have never added any css styling of our own to these elements and yet here they are, all dressed by themselves with no help from us. They grow up so fast.

    Don't let these few unanswered questions haunt you at night. The short and simple answer is: Browser Defaults.

    Did you know that different browsers have their own default style settings? It might be hard to tell at first but take a look at this side-by-side comparison between some popular browsers used today.

    Some aspects may be more difficult to distinguish amongst browsers such as the hyperlinks and headings. However, this becomes much clearer when we look at certain elements such as the checkbox and the drop down menu items. As you saw, these styles are specific to each individual browser.

    Detailed lists of specific browser stylings can be found here:

    • internet explorer
    • firefox
    • google chrome

    The purpose for these browser defaults is so that pages are rendered with more uniformity and consistency if we developers don't specify them ourselves. But what if we didn't want our page to incorporate any of the styles that have been preset? Would we have to go in and change/undo every preset style change that the browser set for them? That seems rather excessive. Fortunately for us there are methods which we can use to make that process more efficient. These methods are called CSS Resets.

    CSS RESETS

    There are a few ways to implement css resets on your page:

    The first is the Universal Selector:

    Universal Selector - How to Use

    In a new css file, we would start this off with an asterisk(*) followed by curly braces {} like so:

    What this does is target every single element on your page. Typically with this method, we would set the margin and padding to 0 to remove any default user agent styles associated with padding and margin.

    *{
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}

    Now that we added these styling changes we will need to link it in our initial html document like so:

        <head>
      <title>Vincent's Webpage</title>
      <link rel="stylesheet" href="/styles.css">
    </head>

    Now that it's been linked, we can take a look at how this affected our webpage (I will be using chrome for any examples moving forward).

    As you can see, all stylings associated with margins and padding have been set to 0. This is a very simple way to reset many styling changes at once.

    The main benefit to this method is that it is a very quick and easy way to remove all formatting. The downside is that if your webpage has a lot of content, this reset would apply to many elements on the page which in turn will affect the speed of the website.

    A better way to reset your file is to reset your style on each element individually. This way, you're only applying the reset styles when they are needed as opposed to every single element that might exist out there which will result in faster speeds on your site.

    ERIC MEYER'S CSS RESET

    Eric Meyer's version of CSS Reset is one of most widely used and popular implementations of this technique.

    Eric Meyers CSS RESET TEMPLATE

    Eric Meyer's Website

    Eric Meyer's CSS Reset - How to Use

    Eric Meyer's reset template is essentially a "plug-and-play". You can just copy it into a css file and link it to your html doc.

        <head>
      <title>Vincent's Webpage</title>
      <link rel="stylesheet" href="/reset.css">
      <link rel="stylesheet" href="/styles.css">
    </head>

    NOTE: It is very important that you place the link before your own style.css link. This is because the page is rendered from top-down, meaning you want to reset your css BEFORE you add your own styling to it.

    For the purposes of this example, our previous styles.css has been commented out to show what reset.css will do to our webpage.

    From there on, you can design and style the page the way you want.

    To summarize CSS Reset, it is an effective way of resetting the styling of HTML elements down to a consistent baseline. This will give the developer a peace of mind knowing that any user's viewing their page via different browsers will not be affected. Performing a css reset is by no means a requirement, but as you go on to develop webpages in the future, it is a great technique to understand in order to save you much time and headaches.

    While CSS reset is a good thing to learn, it doesn't come without its own flaws, such as being hard/nearly impossible to debug. It is also non-modular, meaning that there are no section breaks in styles. This is where Normalize.css comes into play.

    NORMALIZE.CSS

    Normalize.css is a tool developed by Nicolas Gallagher & Jonathan Neal as an alternative to CSS resets.

    Nicolas Gallagher

    Jonathan Neal

    An important distinguishing feature of Normalize.css from CSS resets is that it doesn't aim to completely create a clean slate when it comes to styling, rather it's goal is to maintain a consistent style across different browsers. Instead of resetting all the preset styles, it preserves the useful defaults.

    There are many reasons why one would wish to use this tool over a css reset:

    • Preserved Defaults
      To elaborate on what preserved defaults entails, normalize.css keeps you from having to re-style what you've already reset from using a css reset.

    • Less Clutter
      When it comes to your debugging tools, reset css usually has a long inheritence chain that can just be an absolute nightmare to stare at.

    • Modular Unlike css resets, Normalize.css is modular, meaning that your project is divided into separate sections, making it much easier for you to pick and choose which styles to apply to each of them.
    • Well Documented This project was created to help teach users how separate browsers render their elements by default. Each section of their code has comments to describe its purpose specific to each browser.


    This is just a small snippet to show you what I mean. The repository is very intuitive and helps to provide the user with insight into how it works.

    Supported Browsers

    Currently, normalize.css supports the following browsers:

    • Chrome
    • Edge
    • Firefox ESR+
    • Internet Explorer 10+
    • Safari 8+
    • Opera

    Notable users of Normalize.css

    • Twitter
    • TweetDeck
    • GitHub
    • SoundCloud
    • Guardian
    • Medium
    • Boostrap

    Normalize.css - How to Use

    The repository for normalize.css can be found here.

    Once forked, you can add the link of your file location to your index.html as we've done previously with our reset.css and styles.css files.

    From there, you have two options:

    1. You can modify the preset settings inside of the normalize.css files to your liking.
    2. Leave normalize.css untouched and overwrite those changes in your own .css file. Just be sure to add your .css file under the normalize.css file in your html document.

    TO SUMMARIZE

    Reset CSS resets all styles that come with a browser's user agent. It can however, be excessive as it may make many unnecessary overwrites in the styling. It can be difficult to identify bugs and it is non-modular. You can create your own reset by using the universal selector reset or you can utilize Eric Meyer's template.

    Normalize.css doesn't affect all default stylings. It will instead preserve the useful ones. It corrects bugs and common browser inconsistencies and is well documented and intuitive to use.

    IN CONCLUSION

    Both CSS Reset and Normalize.css have their benefits. It's not necessarily better to use one over the other but it's essential to understand the differences in order to decide which route you wish to take when designing your webpage. Reset CSS is essentially a clean slate, allowing you freedom to style your page from the ground up whereas Normalize.css helps break it down into sections for more modularity.

    Both help to achieve the same goal - to get default stylings out of the way to give you creative freedom to style your page the way you wish to.

Items.Title: 工厂函数与构造函数 | Factory Functions vs Constructors
Items.Description:
发表于要掌握工厂函数和构造函数的概念，首先要了解函数和对象。

如果您要创建大量具有相似属性和功能的对象，则可能会很乏味且重复。 我们要牢记原则或保持我们的代码干燥，它代表“不要重复自己”。 有一些函数模式可以帮助我们编写更短、更简洁、更高效的代码。 工厂函数和构造函数可以做到这一点，但你应该使用哪一个呢？ 让我们更深入地了解一下工厂函数和构造函数是什么以及它们可以做什么。

什么是工厂功能？

工厂功能可以被认为是一个实际的工厂，它吸收原材料并及时生产大量产品。 另一方面，工厂函数接受特定的输入，使用这些输入来创建一个新对象。 那么，这对我们有何用处呢？ 我们可以简单地单独创建每个对象，但这可能需要一些时间。 如果您正在创建具有相同属性和不同值的对象，那么创建一个工厂函数可以加快此过程。 它使我们的代码干燥（不要重复自己）。

 const car1 = { make: 'Toyota', model: 'Tacoma', year: 2018, fuelType: 'gas', bodyType: 'mid-size pick-up truck', getFullName() { return `${this.year} ${this.make} ${this.model}`; } } console.log(car1.getFullName()); // => 2018 Toyota Tacoma

在上面的示例中，我们创建了一个描述特定汽车的对象。 现在让我们制作一个与此类似的对象。

 const car2 = { make: 'Tesla', model: 'Model S', year: 2018, fuelType: 'electric', bodyType: 'sedan', getFullName() { return `${this.year} ${this.make} ${this.model}`; } } console.log(car2.getFullName()); // => 2018 Tesla Model S

现在，我可以继续创建更多汽车的对象，但是谁有时间呢，对吧？ 让我们看看这看起来像一个工厂函数。

 function createCar (make, model, year, fuelType, bodyType) { return { make: make, model: model, year: year, fuelType: fuelType, bodyType: bodyType, getFullName() { return `${this.year} ${this.make} ${this.model}`; } } }

我们现在有一个为我们创建新对象的工厂函数。 现在我们要做的就是传入数据并让工厂函数完成它的工作。

 function createCar (make, model, year, fuelType, bodyType) { return { make: make, model: model, year: year, fuelType: fuelType, bodyType: bodyType, getFullName() { return `${this.year} ${this.make} ${this.model}`; } } } const car1 = createCar('Toyota', 'Tacoma', 2018, 'gas', 'mid-size pick-up truck'); const car2 = createCar('Tesla', 'Model S', 2018, 'electric', 'sedan'); console.log(car1.getFullName()); // => 2018 Toyota Tacoma console.log(car2.getFullName()); // => 2018 Tesla Model S

您可以通过使用工厂函数而不是重复代码来构建任意数量的对象。

什么是构造函数？

构造函数是另一种 javascript 模式，与工厂函数非常相似。虽然与工厂函数不同，构造函数实际上并不返回对象。 要创建具有相同属性的不同对象，我们需要使用关键字“new”和关键字“this”。 话虽如此，让我们看看构造函数是如何直观地工作的。



原文:

b.chau504

Posted on Apr 24

To grasp the concept of factory functions and constructors, you must first understand functions and objects.

If you're creating numerous amounts of objects with similar properties and functionality, it can be tedious and repetitive. We want to keep in mind the principle or keep our code D.R.Y., which stands for "Don't Repeat Yourself". There are function patterns that can help us write shorter, cleaner, more efficient code. Factory functions and constructor functions can do just that, but which one should you use? Let's dive a little deeper into what factory functions and constructors are and what they can do.

What are factory functions?

Factory functions can be thought of like an actual factory that takes in raw materials and produces numerous of products in a timely manner. Factory functions, on the other hand, takes in specific inputs, uses those inputs to create a new object. So, how can this actually be useful to us? We can simply create each object individually, but that might take some time. If you are creating objects with the same properties and different values, then creating one factory function can make this process quicker. It makes our code D.R.Y.(Don't Repeat Yourself).

const car1 = {
    make: 'Toyota',
    model: 'Tacoma',
    year: 2018,
    fuelType: 'gas', 
    bodyType: 'mid-size pick-up truck',
    getFullName() {
        return `${this.year} ${this.make} ${this.model}`;
    }
}

console.log(car1.getFullName()); // => 2018 Toyota Tacoma

In the example above, we create an object describing a specific car. Now let's make a similar object to this one.

const car2 = {
    make: 'Tesla', 
    model: 'Model S',
    year: 2018,
    fuelType: 'electric',
    bodyType: 'sedan',
    getFullName() {
        return `${this.year} ${this.make} ${this.model}`;
    }
}

console.log(car2.getFullName()); // => 2018 Tesla Model S

Now, I can continue creating objects of more cars, but who has time for that, right? Let's see how this would look like as a factory function.

function createCar (make, model, year, fuelType, bodyType) {
    return {
        make: make, 
        model: model, 
        year: year, 
        fuelType: fuelType, 
        bodyType: bodyType,
        getFullName() {
            return `${this.year} ${this.make} ${this.model}`;
        }
    }
}

We now have a factory function that creates new objects for us. Now all we have to do is pass in data and let the factory function do its thing.

function createCar (make, model, year, fuelType, bodyType) {
    return {
        make: make, 
        model: model, 
        year: year, 
        fuelType: fuelType, 
        bodyType: bodyType,
        getFullName() {
            return `${this.year} ${this.make} ${this.model}`;
        }
    }
}

const car1 = createCar('Toyota', 'Tacoma', 2018, 'gas', 'mid-size pick-up truck');
const car2 = createCar('Tesla', 'Model S', 2018, 'electric', 'sedan');

console.log(car1.getFullName()); // => 2018 Toyota Tacoma
console.log(car2.getFullName()); // => 2018 Tesla Model S

You may build any number of objects by utilizing the factory function instead of repeating code.

What are constructors?

A constructor function is another javascript pattern, that is very similar to factory functions.Though unlike factory functions, constructor functions do not actually return an object. To create different object with the same properties, we would need to use the keyword "new" and the keyword "this". With that being said, let's see how constructor functions work visually.

Items.Title: 您的图标应该是 SVG 文件。 这就是为什么。 | Your Icons should probably be SVG files. Here’s why.
Items.Description:
阅读本文后，您可能再也不会使用 .PNG 文件作为图标了。
第一件事。

什么是 .SVG 文件？

与由像素组成的光栅文件（即 JPG 和 PNG）不同，像SVG 这样的矢量图形无论您制作多大或多小都始终保持其分辨率。 不再有模糊的图标！ 您不必担心 SVG 在某些浏览器中会失去其视觉质量，或者当您调整它们的大小以显示在不同的位置时。

基本 SVG 文件的文件大小通常比 PNG 或 JPG 小，它们是由大量像素构建的，而不是数学算法。

由于 SVG 文件将文本视为文本，因此屏幕阅读器可以扫描 SVG 图像中包含的任何单词。 因此，您的网站对于需要帮助阅读网页的人来说更容易访问和有用。 搜索引擎还可以读取和索引 SVG 图像文本。

如果您有兴趣了解更多信息，请参阅 Adob​​e 的 SVG 深入介绍：这里。

现在是有趣的部分……将 SVG 添加到您的项目中。

如何将 SVG 添加到您的网站：

您可以将 SVG 直接插入到 HTML 文件中，并使用 CSS 轻松调整它们。

让我们一步一步地分解它：

第 1 步：创建 SVG 文件或查找 SVG 文件：
创建 SVG 时，最好使用 Adob​​e Illustrator 等矢量图形编辑器。 不是设计师； 不用担心。 您可以在以下网站上找到免费的 SVG 和/或购买 SVG： The Noun Project或Icons8 。

我通过 Adob​​e Illustrator 保存了这个示例 SVG。

第 2 步：获取 SVG 代码：
获取 SVG 文件代码的最简单方法是右键单击文件并在简单的文本编辑器（如 TextEdit）中打开它。 我还将在我的 svg 标记中将属性 id 更改为“icon”，但请随意为您的 id 指定任何您喜欢的名称。 像这样： <svg id=”icon”… ></svg>现在，选择从 SVG 打开标签到结束标签<svg id=”icon”…. ></svg> 第 3 步：将 SVG 代码添加到项目中：
有几种方法可以做到这一点。 因此，如果您有一种将元素添加到 html 文件正文的首选方法，请随时离开脚本。 特别是如果您希望将 SVG 放在特定的<div>中。

但为简单起见，我将（在 Mac 上为 CMD + V 或在 Windows 上为 CTRL + V）我的 SVG 代码直接粘贴到我的 html 文件的正文标记中： <body> (paste here) </body> 。

 <body id="body"> <svg id="icon" data-name="Layer 1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24"> <path d="M12,.92A11.08,11.08,0,1,0,23.08,12,11.08,11.08,0,0,0,12,.92Zm5.52,15.191.41,1.41L12,13.41,7.89,17.52,6.48,16.11,10.59,12,6.48,7.89,7.89,6.48,12,10.59l4.11-4.11,1.41,1.41L13.41,12Z" /> </svg> </body>

这是您在预览 html 文件时应该看到的内容。

第 4 步：设置 SVG 代码的样式：
你可以用任何你喜欢的方式来设计你的 SVG。 选择适合您喜欢的颜色、尺寸、属性等。 我要将我的 SVG 的大小更改为 5em。 在您的 CSS 文件中，输入：'#icon'，它在您的 html 文件中的 SVG 代码中引用 id="icon"，然后在大括号内输入：'width: 5em;' 或您希望图标的任何尺寸宽度。

这是您在预览 html 文件时应该看到的内容。

我将在项目的 CSS 文件中将 SVG 的填充颜色更改为蓝色。

 #icon { width: 5em; fill: blue; }

同样，这是您在预览 html 文件时应该看到的内容。

你有它。 易榨柠檬汁。

使用 CSS 向 SVG 文件添加悬停效果或动画也非常简单。 设计、实验和迭代。

如果您喜欢这篇文章，请给它点赞、分享或评论。
感谢阅读。



原文:

After reading this, you may never use a .PNG file for an icon again.
First things first.

What is an .SVG file?

An SVG (Scaleable Vector Graphic) is a popular, web-friendly file format.

The advantages SVG files have over PNGs or JPGs:

Unlike raster files (i.e. JPGs and PNGs), which are made up of pixels, vector graphics like SVGs always maintain their resolution no matter how large or small you make them. No more blurry icons! You need not worry about SVGs losing their visual quality in certain browsers or when you resize them to appear in different places.

Basic SVG files often have smaller file sizes than PNGs or JPGs, which are built from tons of pixels as opposed to mathematical algorithms.

Because SVG files treat text as text, screen readers can scan any words contained in SVG images. Therefore, your website is more accessible and useful for people who need help reading webpages. Search engines can also read and index SVG image text.

If you’re interested in learning more, here's an SVG deep dive from Adobe: here.

Now for the fun part… adding SVGs to your project.

How to Add SVGs to your website:

You can insert SVGs directly into your HTML file and easily adjust them with CSS.

Let’s break it down step-by-step:

STEP 1: Create an SVG file or Find an SVG file:
When creating an SVG, it’s best-practice to use a vector graphics editor like Adobe Illustrator. Not a designer; not to worry. You can find free SVGs and/or purchase SVGs on sites like: The Noun Project or Icons8.

I’m saving this example SVG via Adobe Illustrator.

Step 2: Get the SVG code:
The easiest way to get your SVG file’s code is simply to right-click the file and open it in a simple text editor like TextEdit. I’m also going to change the attribute id to “icon” inside of my svg tags, but feel free to give your id any name you like. Like so: <svg id=”icon”… ></svg> Now, select all of the text starting from the SVG open tag through to the close tag <svg id=”icon”…. ></svg> and copy it (CMD + C on a Mac or CTRL + C on Windows).

Step 3: Adding your SVG code into your project:
There are a few ways to do this. So, feel free to go off-script here if you have a preferred method for adding elements to the body of your html file. Especially if you’re looking to place your SVG inside a specific <div>.

But for simplicity's sake, I’m going to paste (CMD + V on a Mac or CTRL + V on Windows) my SVG code directly inside the body tag of my html file: <body> (paste here) </body>.

<body id="body">
  <svg id="icon" data-name="Layer 1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
    <path

d="M12,.92A11.08,11.08,0,1,0,23.08,12,11.08,11.08,0,0,0,12,.92Zm5.52,15.191.41,1.41L12,13.41,7.89,17.52,6.48,16.11,10.59,12,6.48,7.89,7.89,6.48,12,10.59l4.11-4.11,1.41,1.41L13.41,12Z" />
  </svg>
</body>

This is what you should see when you preview your html file.

Step 4: Styling your SVG code:
You can style your SVG any way you’d like. Choose the colors, size, attributes, etc. that fit your fancy. I’m going to change the size of my SVG to 5em. (Yes, I know that's too big. Ignore that... it's for screenshot visibility.)

In your CSS file, type: '#icon', which references id="icon" in your SVG code in your html file, and within the curly brackets, type: 'width: 5em;' or whatever size width you'd like your icon to be.

This is what you should see when you preview your html file.

I'm going to change the fill color of my SVG to blue in my project’s CSS file.

#icon {
  width: 5em;
  fill: blue;
}

Again, this is what you should see when you preview your html file.

And there you have it. Easy-peasy lemon squeezy.

It’s also pretty simple to add hover effects or animations to your SVG file with CSS. Design, experiment and iterate away.

If you liked this article, give it a like, share, or comment.
Thanks for reading.
